title: Different-compound final-stop opening
summary: Specialize final-stop window entry to cases where the newly feasible tyre is not the current dry compound.
rationale: General fresh-slick feasibility worked well, and dry-compound obligation variants suggested that changing compound can be strategically distinct from fitting a fresher copy of the same compound.
implementation_hint: After computing newly feasible fresh slicks, add whether any newly feasible option differs from the current `Compound`, the softest different newly feasible slick, and the best headroom among different-compound openings. Use only current compound and current-row remaining-distance estimates.
expected_effect: Small expected ROC AUC gain with medium confidence; likely complementary to rule-state features.
risk: May be redundant if AutoGluon already learns interactions between current compound and per-compound margins.
